heading: Commissioned officers: retention in active status while assigned to Selective Service System or serving as United States property and fiscal officers
label: 12647 Commissioned officers: retention in active status while assigned to Selective Service System or serving as United States property and fiscal officers
text: Notwithstanding chapters 843, 1407, and 1409 of this title, a reserve commissioned officer, other than a commissioned warrant officer, who is assigned to the Selective Service System or who is a property and fiscal officer appointed, designated, or detailed under , may be retained in an active status in that assignment or position until he becomes 62 years of age.section 708 of title 32
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)100750:1181(1) (as applicable to 50:1202).50:1202., §§ 102(1) (as applicable to § 212), 212, , 1153.Sept. 3, 1954, ch. 125768 Stat. 1149The words “this title” are substituted for the words “this chapter”, since the provisions of this title requiring transfer from an active status are based on the source statute for this section (the Reserve Officer Personnel Act of 1954).

Amendments2018— substituted “chapters 843, 1407, and 1409” for “chapters 573, 1407, and 1409”.Pub. L. 115–2322008— substituted “62 years” for “60 years”.Pub. L. 110–4171994—, substituted “573, 1407, and 1409” for “337, 363, 573, 837, and 863”.Pub. L. 103–337, § 1675(d)(4), renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 103–337, § 1662(h)(2)section 1007 of this title1960— inserted “or serving as United States property and fiscal officers” in section catchline, and inserted provisions in text authorizing retention of reserve commissioned officers who are property and fiscal officers, appointed, designated, or detailed under .Pub. L. 86–559section 708 of title 32
